rows.description: Dec. 26 to Francis King by Bill
rows.notes: Clear handwriting. Pounds (1) and shillings (9) are unambiguous. Pence appears to be 6 with 'ob' fraction symbol for half-penny. Column alignment is good.

rows.description: to 1/2 Dozen of 6d. paid for hay & hanging lanyon
rows.notes: No pounds. Shillings (2) and pence (6) are clearly written and aligned with column lines. Valid currency values.
Clear reading. Shillings (1) and zero pence are unambiguous. Standard notation for 'Corn price' entry.
Very clear. Pounds (1), shillings (0), pence (0). Well-aligned numbers. No ambiguity.
Clear alignment. Pounds (8), shillings (13), pence (6) are all within valid ranges. Handwriting is legible.

rows.description: 17 to Mrs Weston four-bean Interval Due at Xmas 1743 & at Easter upon bond by common consent
rows.notes: Longer entry with date reference (1743). Pounds (12), shillings (8), pence (0) are clearly visible. Some ink variation but figures are unambiguous.
Simple entry. Shillings (1), zero pence clearly visible. Consistent with prior 'Corn price' entries.

rows.description: to Mrs Ralph one shilion of Imp. Paper
rows.notes: Shillings (12) and pence (6) are clear. 'Shilion' appears to be 'Shilden' or similar (uncertain transcription of description, but amounts are clear).
Clear handwriting. Pounds (1), shillings (5), pence (2) are all unambiguous and within valid ranges.
Pounds (13) and shillings (17) are clear. 'badily' likely 'badily' or similar uncertain word, but amounts are unambiguous. Shillings (17) is valid.
Clean reading. No pounds, shillings (8), pence (6). Well-aligned with column boundaries.
Large amount. Pounds (41) is clear but substantial. Shillings (8) and pence (4) are unambiguous. Column alignment confirms large pound value.

rows.description: May 19 & 1/4 wine to entertain Mrs Widnot in P.t.
rows.notes: Shillings (1) and pence (6) are clear. Text mentions wine and entertainment. Some handwriting cursiveness but numbers are legible.

rows.description: June 12 to Mrs Bogle's works for y Library
rows.notes: Clear and well-aligned. Pounds (5), shillings (5), pence (0). 'Bogle's works' likely refers to a library service or repair.

rows.description: July 7 to Mrs Combes her Bill
rows.notes: Simple, clear entry. No pounds, shillings (4), zero pence. Excellent alignment and legibility.

rows.description: 10 to Mr. Well Tallet by Plate money
rows.notes: Clear reading. Pounds (8), zero shillings, zero pence. 'Tallet' or 'Tallet' refers to a loft structure. Amount is unambiguous.

rows.description: Aug 1 to Mr Townsend by Bill
rows.notes: Large amount. Pounds (44), shillings (5) are clear. Pence (11) with apparent 'ob' fraction symbol (0.5) visible. All values valid.

rows.description: 10 to Dr Dexter 2 of year 1742. 1743. 1/2 bid
rows.notes: Complex date reference (spanning years 1742–1743). Pounds (19) and shillings (8) are clear. 'bid' notation unclear but monetary amounts are unambiguous.

rows.description: 20 brought to her & way ye Steurman for Wine to entertaiu Goll. Sent.
rows.notes: Text is somewhat unclear (possibly 'Stuersman' or similar, 'Goll' possibly 'Coll'). However, amounts are clear: no pounds, shillings (3), zero pence.
Standard 'Corn price' entry format. Shillings (1), zero pence. Clear and consistent with other similar entries.

rows.description: 25 to Salisbury Carrier for 2 Boxes from Mr Coade
rows.notes: Clear entry for a carrier service. Shillings (9) and pence (4) are unambiguous. 'Coade' is a known reference name.

rows.description: Oct. 10 to her Horner for Paper, Ink, &c.
rows.notes: Clear supplies entry. Shillings (17) and pence (4) are legible and well-aligned. 'Horner' is the supplier name.

rows.description: to fiddles for binding two Battery Books
rows.notes: Binding service for books. Pounds (1), shillings (4), zero pence are clear. 'Battery' possibly 'Buttery' but amounts are unambiguous.
Brief wine entry. Shillings (16) is clear. Zero pence. The '&t. 5' notation is abbreviated but amount is unambiguous.

rows.description: 20 to Hay Wife for carrying away rubbish
rows.notes: Clear domestic service entry. Pounds (2), shillings (3), zero pence are unambiguous and well-aligned.

Subtotal line showing cumulative sum. Pounds (356) is large but clearly written. Shillings (12) and pence (5) with fraction symbol that appears to be 3/4 farthing. Alignment confirms column positions.
Another subtotal or running balance. Pounds (179), shillings (16), pence (2). Numbers are aligned with column lines but handwriting shows some variation.
Intermediate balance or subtotal. Pounds (90), shillings (7), pence (4) with fraction mark (3/4). Label is partially obscured, but amounts are readable with moderate confidence.

Shillings value is 25, which exceeds the valid maximum of 19. This indicates a likely misalignment of column boundaries OR the value is written differently than expected. Re-examined; may be £94 5s 8¾d with a printing/spacing issue. Flag for supervisor review.
Clear labeling as 'Sum om. Totall.' (Sum total). Pounds (757), shillings (1), pence (8¾) are unambiguous. Large pound value is within plausible range for cumulative total.
Account statement line. Text is 'Dr blakly qua evrant' (possibly 'Dr Blake qua everant'). Amounts £51 1s 8¾d are clearly written. This appears to be a debtor/creditor notation.

Text appears to reference a date (1739) and receipt note. The £1739 appears very large; may be a date field rather than a pound amount. Shillings (2) and pence (9) are clear. Uncertain interpretation of structure.
Latin abbreviations: 'ne Delt. Dno Comput.' (accounting notation). Amounts £41 18s 2¾d are clearly visible. This appears to be a final computation or settlement line.
Signature block or attestation. No amounts. Legible handwriting. Identifies Jacobus December as Rector—likely the certifying official.
Signature attestation. No amounts. Identifies Jespish Webber (or Jaspish/Jasper) as Church Warden. Legible.
Third attestation. Names are partially unclear ('Cogkpley' may be 'Cockley' or similar; 'J/B' is abbreviation). No amounts. This appears to be another signatory or official.
Abbreviation 'Cav. Webber H.M.' (possibly 'Capt. Webber H.M.'). No amounts. Likely a final attestation or certification by another official.
Very abbreviated text ('shr. Shauly s. Sh.B.'). Possibly 'Sheriff Shauly' or similar. Difficult to read with certainty but clearly part of signature/attestation block. No amounts.
